input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
Here's the migration path:

## 1. Set properties consistently via `Directory.Build.props`

Add (or update) a `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <!-- Switch MSTest to the new platform runner -->
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Output type required by the new runner -->
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

| Property | Why |
|---|---|
| `EnableMSTestRunner` | Replaces the VSTest hosting model with the MSTest runner built on Microsoft.Testing.Platform. |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Lets `dotnet test` discover/run tests through the new platform instead of VSTest. Required on .NET 8 SDK (the SDK doesn't auto-detect the new runner without it). |
| `OutputType` = `Exe` | The new runner compiles each test project as a self-contained executable. Without this you get a build error. |

No package changes are needed — MSTest 3.8.0 already bundles the `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` transport.

## 2. CI command

Replace:

```bash
dotnet test --logger trx
```

With:

```bash
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

The command itself stays the same — `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` ensures `dotnet test` routes through the new platform. Adding `--results-directory` is recommended because each test project is now its own executable and may write results to its own output directory; pinning a shared directory keeps CI artifact collection simple.

## 3. Verify

After the changes, run:

```bash
dotnet build
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

You should see each test project launch as its own process (look for `Microsoft.Testing.Platform v…` in the output rather than the old `VSTest` banner).
data.reasoningText: The user wants to migrate 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform on .NET 8. Let me provide a clear, concise answer based on my knowledge of this migration.
